latest.title: Sale of WorkiQ Trademarks
latest.content:
2 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1560Z
ActiveOps PLC
02 April 2026
THE INFORMATION CONTAINED WITHIN THIS ANNOUNCEMENT IS DEEMED TO CONSTITUTE INSIDE INFORMATION AS STIPULATED UNDER THE MARKET ABUSE REGULATION (EU NO. 596/2014) WHICH IS PART OF UK LAW BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018. UPON THE PUBLICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT, THIS INSIDE INFORMATION IS NOW CONSIDERED TO BE IN THE PUBLIC DOMAIN.
02 April 2026
ActiveOps plc
("ActiveOps", the "Company" or the "Group")
Sale of WorkiQ Trademarks
ActiveOps plc (AIM: AOM), a leading provider of Decision Intelligence software for service operations, announces that it has agreed to sell to Microsoft Corporation the trademarks that it holds in the UK, US, Australia and EU to the WorkiQ name.
The consideration to be received by the Group for the sale of the WorkiQ trademarks is USD$10 million (approximately £7.5 million) in cash.
The Board considers that the sale of the WorkiQ trademarks has no impact on the ActiveOps Decision Intelligence software suite of products, and the WorkiQ product will be rebranded in due course.
The proceeds from the sale will provide the Group with additional capital, the Board is considering the best utilisation of this capital and will provide an update alongside the year end results announcement in early July.
For more information, please contact:
ActiveOps
Via Alma
Richard Jeffery, Executive Chair
www.activeops.com
Emma Salthouse, Chief Financial Officer
Investec Bank plc
+44 (0)20 7597 5970
Nominated Adviser and Joint Broker
Patrick Robb / Nick Prowting / James Smith
Canaccord Genuity Limited
+44 (0)20 7523 8000
Joint Broker
Simon Bridges / Harry Gooden / Harry Rees
Alma Strategic Communications
+ 44(0) 203 405 0205
Caroline Forde / Louisa El-Ahwal
The person responsible for arranging release of this announcement on behalf of ActiveOps plc is Emma Salthouse, Chief Financial Officer.
About ActiveOps
ActiveOps is a Software as a Service business, dedicated to helping organisations create more value from their service operations. ActiveOps' Decision Intelligence software solutions are specifically designed to support leaders with the vast number of decisions they make daily in the running their operations. Our customers make better decisions and consume less time and effort making them. The outcomes are significantly improved turnaround times and double-digit improvements in productivity with backlogs of work materially reduced. Customers also leverage the capacity created to invest in transformation and development, and more efficiently utilise resources.
The Company's AI-powered SaaS solutions are underpinned by over 15 years of operational data and its AOM methodology which is proven to enhance cross departmental decision-making.
The Company has approximately 280 employees, serving a global base of enterprise customers from offices in the UK, Ireland, USA, Canada, Australia, India, and South Africa. The Group's customers are predominantly in the banking, insurance, healthcare administration and business process outsourcing (BPO) sectors, including Nationwide, TD Bank, Elevance and Xchanging.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact
rns@lseg.com
or visit
www.rns.com
.RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. .

Director/PDMR Dealing

ActiveOps' Decision Intelligence software solutions are specifically designed to support leaders with the vast number of decisions they make daily in the running their operations. Our customers make better decisions and consume less time and effort making them. The outcomes are significantly improved turnaround times and double-digit improvements in productivity with backlogs of work materially reduced. Customers also leverage the capacity created to invest in transformation and development, and more efficiently utilise resources.

About ActiveOps
ActiveOps is a Software as a Service business, dedicated to helping organisations create more value from their service operations. ActiveOps' Decision Intelligence software solutions are specifically designed to support leaders with the vast number of decisions they make daily in the running their operations. Our customers make better decisions and consume less time and effort making them. The outcomes are significantly improved turnaround times and double-digit improvements in productivity with backlogs of work materially reduced. Customers also leverage the capacity created to invest in transformation and development, and more efficiently utilise resources.
The Company's AI-powered SaaS solutions are underpinned by 15+ years of operational data and its AOM methodology which is proven to enhance cross departmental decision-making.
The Company has approximately 280 employees, serving a global base of enterprise customers from offices in the UK, Ireland, USA, Canada, Australia, India, and South Africa. The Group's customers are predominantly in the banking, insurance, healthcare administration and business process outsourcing (BPO) sectors, including Nationwide, TD Bank, Elevance and Xchanging.
.

ActiveOps Plc

Total Voting Rights and Share Capital
ActiveOps plc (AIM: AOM), a leading provider of Decision Intelligence for service operations,
makes the following announcement in accordance with Rule 5.6.1 of the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency rules.
As of 27th February 2026, the total number of ordinary shares of £0.001 each in issue is 71,449,528. Therefore, the total number of voting rights in ActiveOps plc is 71,449,528.

company_30s.what_they_do: ActiveOps builds cloud‑based workforce management software that lets businesses schedule staff, track time, and analyse labour costs.  It targets retail, hospitality, logistics and other service‑heavy sectors that need to optimise shift planning and compliance.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The workforce‑management market is growing as companies look to cut labour costs and improve productivity.  ActiveOps offers a SaaS platform that can replace legacy spreadsheets and on‑premise systems, giving it a competitive edge in speed and scalability.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still scaling – revenue is modest and it is likely burning cash to grow its product and sales team.  It has a small, active investor base and is preparing for the next annual general meeting, which could signal a new direction or fresh funding.

rns_narrative: The latest filings show a mix of asset divestments (sale of WorkiQ trademarks) and governance updates (director shareholdings and voting rights).  These moves suggest the management is streamlining the business and tightening its balance sheet.

The AGM is scheduled for September, giving the board a chance to present a refreshed strategy, new contracts, or a fundraising round.  The series of announcements also hints at a potential cost‑cutting push and a focus on core products.
Overall, the company appears to be stabilising its operations rather than expanding aggressively, which could reduce risk but also limit upside if growth stalls.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: quiet optimism – a few bullish voices about the AGM, but little overall chatter
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders see the upcoming AGM and the director’s share purchases as a sign that management is confident and may announce new deals or a funding round.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: A handful of users point out the company’s weak website and past volatility, warning that without clear revenue growth the stock could stay low.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has been flat for months, but the small uptick in bullish posts ahead of the AGM shows a slow shift toward cautious optimism.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat – no public search activity, which means a good surprise potential if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: AGM in September – management could unveil new contracts, a funding round or a product roadmap.
Any RNS that details a partnership or a revenue‑generating deal in the retail or hospitality space.
A spike in trading volume that coincides with a positive announcement would confirm buying interest.
If the company releases a quarterly report showing an uptick in ARR or a reduction in churn, that would be a strong sign.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to monitor over the next 2–3 months include: a sudden rise in daily volume, the stock breaking through its 200‑day moving average, and any mention of new customer wins in the press or on social media.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early, pre‑bounce phase of a potential recovery.  It has not yet shown clear signs of a bottom, so patience is required.  A breakout above the current support zone would be the first step toward mean reversion.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the AGM reveals a new partnership or a modest funding round, the stock could climb 20–30% as investors reassess its valuation.  A deep crash has not yet been confirmed, so the upside is moderate but the risk is high.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Without a clear revenue story, the company could remain in a low‑volatility, low‑price range.  The risk of a continued cash burn and potential delisting is real if the upcoming announcements fail to deliver traction.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside of 20–30% versus a downside that could be as little as the current trading range.  The asymmetry is mild; the trade is more about timing than huge gains.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the stock closes above the 200‑day moving average and volume is at least 1.5× the 20‑day average.  Size the position so that a 5% drop would hit your stop, and set a target at the next resistance level, roughly 15–20% above entry.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for either a positive RNS about a new deal or a spike in volume that confirms buying interest.  The trigger could be a 30% jump in daily volume on a day the price is near the 200‑day MA.
playbook.if_passing: If the company fails to announce any new revenue or partnership by the AGM, or if it issues a negative RNS (e.g., a downgrade or a significant debt raise), consider exiting to avoid prolonged downside.

ai_insights.bottom_line: AOM is a workforce‑management SaaS company that is still stabilising its operations; a positive AGM or new partnership could spark a modest bounce, but the upside is limited and the risk of continued low valuation remains.
